code_usability.detail: Category 4 Academic Writing — no code generated

security.note: Input Validation 2/4 — no formal out-of-scope refusal template

agent_specific.note: Escape Hatches 2/4 — vague input handled but no explicit scope-refusal section

assertions.text: Output follows the 6-section template from assets/output-template.md
assertions.note: All 6 sections present in correct order

assertions.text: All 5 team-specific bullets present for mixed audience
assertions.note: Clinical, Wet-lab, Bioinformatics, Product, Management all included

assertions.text: Evidence boundary explicitly stated
assertions.note: Observational limitation and lack of functional validation clearly stated

assertions.text: Key finding quantified with numbers from input
assertions.note: 2.4-fold upregulation and HR 1.8 (95% CI 1.2–2.7) cited correctly from source

assertions.text: No statistics fabricated beyond what was provided in input
assertions.note: All numbers traceable to input abstract

inputs.label: Clinical-only audience, Phase II RCT gastric cancer

assertions.text: Output restricts team bullets to clinical audience only
assertions.note: Only clinical bullet present; four other audiences correctly omitted

assertions.text: Phase II limitations prominently stated
assertions.note: Explicitly states phase II, 89 patients — not practice-changing standard of care

assertions.text: Toxicity data (grade 3+ AEs) accurately conveyed
assertions.note: 38% vs. 21% included in evidence boundary section

assertions.text: No recommendation to change standard of care without OS data
assertions.note: Explicitly notes OS benefit not demonstrated

assertions.text: Next step references phase III with OS endpoint and biomarker stratification
assertions.note: Concrete, appropriate — not generic further research needed

inputs.label: Multi-modal scRNA-seq/ATAC/spatial with negative TCGA survival validation

assertions.text: Negative TCGA survival result (p=0.41) clearly communicated without downplaying
assertions.note: Featured prominently in evidence boundary section and management bullet

assertions.text: Complex multi-modal methods translated without fabricating causal mechanism
assertions.note: WNN described as combined single-cell data; no overclaiming of causality

assertions.text: No translational overclaiming despite interesting mechanistic findings
assertions.note: Product bullet explicitly states not yet translatable

assertions.text: Unspecified audience defaults to mixed with all 5 bullets
assertions.note: Skill default correctly applied per Step 1 instructions

assertions.text: Output accurately represents both positive (14 TFs) and negative (no survival link) components
assertions.note: Both components prominently framed with appropriate epistemic hedging

assertions.text: Output uses management-appropriate plain language throughout
assertions.note: No unexplained technical terms; direct executive framing used consistently

assertions.text: Retrospective validation limitation is stated
assertions.note: Explicitly notes retrospective validation — not a prospectively designed trial

assertions.text: AUC explained in plain language
assertions.note: Described as high accuracy with '1.0 = perfect prediction' gloss

assertions.text: Next step is concrete, not vague
assertions.note: Specific mention of prospective study and industry partnership for companion diagnostic

assertions.text: Single audience (management) correctly implemented
assertions.note: Only management bullet present; no other audience content included

inputs.label: Vague input — metabolic ML findings, no audience specified
inputs.note: Correctly declined to write; requested targeted clarification instead

assertions.text: Skill correctly declines to produce lay summary from vague input and requests clarification
assertions.note: Explicitly invokes skill principle: lay summary on unclear input will be factually imprecise

assertions.text: Clarifying questions align with the 5 core elements defined in Step 2
assertions.note: Questions map to finding, quantification, audience, and study design from the Step 2 table

assertions.text: No lay summary content fabricated from the vague description
assertions.note: No invented metabolic results; refusal is clean

assertions.text: Response provides concrete actionable path for user to unblock
assertions.note: User directed to paste 2-3 sentences from results section

key_strengths: Excellent progressive disclosure: SKILL.md is 108 lines with output template and audience guide externalized to assets/ and references/, keeping context overhead minimal
Explicit pipeline positioning with upstream and downstream skills named — enables clean composability with slide deck, graphical abstract, and reviewer response skills
Robust vague-input handling: skill explicitly instructs Claude to seek clarification rather than produce inaccurate summaries, validated across all 5 test inputs
Granular 5-audience differentiation with distinct language registers per team type in references/audience-guide.md, without bloating SKILL.md
Strong evidence boundary design: negative findings, phase II limitations, and retrospective validation all handled with correct epistemic framing

recommendations.title: Missing formal Input Validation / out-of-scope escape hatch
recommendations.problem: No explicit out-of-scope refusal template exists. A user asking for a cover letter, press release, or patient consent document could trigger this skill inappropriately.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill correctly handles in-scope edge cases (vague input, missing audience) but has no formal mechanism to decline clearly off-scope requests.
recommendations.fix: Add an ## Input Validation section above References defining valid inputs (research abstracts, key findings, study summaries) and providing a refusal template for off-scope requests such as press releases, clinical consent forms, or social media copy.

recommendations.title: No handling of non-English input
recommendations.problem: Step 1 implicitly assumes English-language input. International teams submitting abstracts in Chinese, Japanese, or German will get inconsistent behavior.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill was designed for English-language workflows; multilingual use case not addressed.
recommendations.fix: Add one sentence to Step 1: 'If the input is not in English, produce the lay summary in the same language as the input, unless the user specifies otherwise.'

recommendations.title: Step 2 internal extraction not surfaced to user before writing
recommendations.problem: Step 2 asks Claude to internally map five core elements before writing, but there is no instruction to show this mapping or flag missing elements to the user before proceeding to Step 3.
recommendations.root_cause: Extraction is a silent pre-writing check; its outputs are not exposed to allow user correction.
recommendations.fix: Add to Step 2: 'If one or more elements cannot be extracted, list the missing elements explicitly and invite the user to fill them in before proceeding to Step 3.'
